attempts.insight: `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` is misleadingly namespaced — despite the `ShortExact.` prefix, it is about plain `ShortComplex.Exact`. The two-step rw chain (`HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` unfolds `ExactAt n` to `(K.sc n).Exact`; then the namespaced lemma unfolds `Exact` to `Function.Exact` on the underlying functions) lands the goal in the exact input shape consumed by `exact_of_isLocalized_span` — saving one mechanical step from iter-062+.

findings.blocker: Extra-degeneracy infrastructure on the slice cover Čech complex. Requires: (1) augmented Čech simplicial object on the slice cover `fun f' => basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1` of `D(f)`; (2) `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` invocation with `i₀ := f` (the cover member `D(f') ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` at `f' = f` providing the terminal section); (3) `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` packaging the extra-degeneracy as a homotopy equivalence with the constant complex; (4) op-passage via `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` from Mathlib's chain-complex form to the project's cochain-complex form; (5) read-off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n` from null-homotopy. The substep is stated as `have h_a : ∀ (f : s), … ExactAt n := by intro f; sorry` at L1713-1722 of `MayerVietoris.lean`.

attempts.strategy: Substep (a) was named as a `have` declaration but its body (the augmented Čech simplicial object construction + extra-degeneracy + op-passage chain) was left as a transient sorry per the iter-061 directive's substep-decomposition guidance.

attempts.insight: The data-bearing per-`f` shape is in place. Iter-062 or iter-063 (depending on prioritisation) will build the augmented Čech object construction + extra-degeneracy invocation. Note that the `f' = f` case yielding `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` provides the terminal section automatically — this is the only point where the choice of slice cover (curried over original index `s`) matters.

next_steps: Substep (a) can wait for iter-063+. Its infrastructure (`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` in the slice cover's `Over D(f)` setting, or its op-dual on `Opens`, plus op-passage from chain to cochain complexes via `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`) is delicate but well-charted by Mathlib. Iter-062 is expected to focus on substep (b) (the data-bearing identification iso) first per the iter-061 prover's recommendation.

findings.blocker: Substep (b)+(c) combined: cochain-level localisation iso + `exact_of_isLocalized_span` ring-base bridge. Requires: (1) constructing the localised Čech short complex at degree n as a `noncomputable def`; (2) a `ShortComplex.Iso` or `LinearEquiv` identifying it with `(slice cover Čech).sc n` via the iter-057 + iter-059 chain (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`, `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`, localisation transitivity `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]`, product-localisation commutation, `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`); (3) `exact_of_isLocalized_span` application across `f ∈ s`; (4) ring-base reconciliation between `k`-linear ModuleCat maps and `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear local-to-global hypothesis. Substep (b)+(c) sorry at L1778 after the iter-061 mechanical `rw` conversion.
